hero.question: LoL: SK Gaming vs GIANTX - Game 2 Winner
hero.prob_take: SK Gaming at 12.5% is essentially priced as a dead man walking, meaning the market expects GIANTX to take Game 2 with near-certainty. The 40-point drop in 24h is noise, but the real question is whether SK can steal a map in a series they're already down in, and that edge is probably worth more than a dime.

daily_take.headline: SK Gaming Collapse Signals Sharp Fade, Not Fair Value at 12.5%
daily_take.deck: SK Gaming's odds tanked 40 points in 24 hours on a single loss, now sitting at 12.5% for Game 2. The market panicked. Smart money doesn't panic after one game, especially in a series where momentum swings are the only constant. GIANTX is a real team, but this price smells like an overcorrection waiting to be arbitraged back up.

sidebar.headline: Iran ground op odds jumped 9 points as escalation rhetoric heated up, but April 2026 is an eternity in geopolitics. Current pricing doesn't reflect the base rate of these conflicts actually stalling out.
sidebar.label: Israel Iran Op: 23.0%

sidebar.headline: Lakers just got smoked and the market dusted them 18 points. Mavericks looked sharp, but 53.5% is overbought on a single statement game before the series really starts.
sidebar.label: Lakers vs Mavericks: 53.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic odds bounced 6 points as shipping data stabilized, yet the $2.8M volume suggests real money is pricing in longer regional tensions. Follow the smart money, not the headlines.
sidebar.label: Strait of Hormuz Normal: 14.5%
